description: A small azure damselfly named after the entomologist Van Someren. Males have blue and black patterning. It is closely related to *Azuragrion somalicum* and similarly little-studied.
behaviour: Little documented. Found near water bodies in its East African range.
distribution: *Azuragrion vansomereni* is known from Kenya, Ethiopia, and Somalia.
habitat: Still and slow-flowing waters in East Africa.
flight_period: Poorly known; likely year-round in suitable habitat.
sources: Atlas of the European Dragonflies and Damselflies, Jean-Pierre Boudot(Editor), Vincent J Kalkman(Editor), Fons Peels(Illustrator)
Field guide to the dragonflies of Britain and Europe, Klaas-Douwe B Dijkstra.
